rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Training platform = Claude Code (CLI + Desktop) + Cowork.
Verify capabilities BEFORE asserting, not after.
rules_evaluated.evidence: line:35 — "`CLAUDE.md` tells Cowork when to use it" — CLAUDE.md auto-load behavior in Cowork is not documented in reference_cowork_capabilities.md. Platform rule 6d documents auto-load for Claude Code sessions; no equivalent entry exists for Cowork tasks. The build-your-system exercise (line 89) asserts the same claim implicitly. Unverified for the Cowork runtime.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Add a verified Cowork CLAUDE.md auto-load entry to reference_cowork_capabilities.md (probe: open a fresh Cowork task on a folder containing CLAUDE.md; ask 'What rules are you following?'; confirm whether the file is auto-read). Then backport the confirmed or corrected phrasing to this line and the build-your-system exercise.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Sessions are the unit of work — write 'start a new session in X,' not 'open Claude Code in X.'
rules_evaluated.evidence:
line:24 — "Open Cowork on that folder." — The Cowork capabilities reference explicitly says: "Don't say 'connect to', 'open at', or 'open in' — those are wrong verbs for Cowork." The correct affordance is "Click **New task** and select `<path>` as the working folder."
line:45 — "Open a new Cowork session on the same folder." — The capabilities reference specifies the Cowork unit is "task" not "session." Curriculum prose at the Cowork seam must use "task" or a generic recast per capabilities doc.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Line 24: replace with 'Click **New task** in Cowork and select this folder as the working folder.' Line 45: replace 'Open a new Cowork session' with 'Start a new Cowork task' (or 'Click **New task** in Cowork and select the same folder').

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Cowork is sandboxed to its connected working directories.
Cowork capability surface — UI surfaces subagent reads; no session rename in Cowork or Desktop.
Every prompt block gets a one-sentence action lead-in.
rules_evaluated.evidence: The section intro ('When both exercises are done, ask Claude to summarize what your folder can now do.') is separated from the prompt block by the Session widget and a setup instruction ('Open a new Cowork session on the same folder.'). Per the canonical shape (rule 3), the lead-in must be immediately before the **Prompt** label. The intervening setup line reads as a mechanical instruction, not the semantic action lead-in.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Move the semantic lead-in to immediately precede the prompt marker, or add a second lead-in sentence directly above the marker ('Ask Claude to summarize what this folder can do and which habits are worth carrying forward.') after the setup instruction.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Markdown shout inside fenced prompt blocks is dead weight in Claude Code.
Preamble before action — design around it.
rules_evaluated.evidence: The prompt in cb-homework-folder-summary.md has 4 named question sub-steps under 'Tell me:' (what I can do, reusable patterns, rule/habit worth carrying, what to run through a separate checker). Rule 17's structural trigger fires at 4+ named sub-steps. No in-fence 'No preamble.' suppressor is present, and no body primer appears after the prompt block (the next section is '## Follow-up' with 'The live session is over.').
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Either add 'No preamble.' as the final line of the prompt in the registry file cb-homework-folder-summary.md (requires maintainer approval per rule 22), or add a one-line body primer immediately after the {{prompt:cb-homework-folder-summary}} marker: 'Claude will narrate briefly before listing; skim past the opening sentence and read the bullets.'

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Prompts live in the registry; module files reference them by marker.

platform_claims_checked.claim: `CLAUDE.md` tells Cowork when to use it
Open a new Cowork session on the same folder.
Everything you save in these exercises stays there.
